title: Luffy’s Grief and the ASL Past: Key Exchange
description: In the Post-War / ASL Flashback storyline, this section centers on Magellan, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12, Unknown person #9, Unknown person #7 as the scene develops across pages 9–12.
scene: This scene advances the Post-War / ASL Flashback conflict through Magellan, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12, Unknown person #9, focusing on the immediate stakes of luffy’s grief and the asl past: key exchange.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: He's taken an impossible amount of damage.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: That's right!!! Strawhat tried his hardest!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Damn it, hang in there, Strawhat !!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: I had no obligation to help him.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: You're TraFalgar Law, from North Blue, Right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: If you don't sleep, you'll die.

dialogue.speaker: Magellan
dialogue.text: My heart won't calm down... it's futile...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #21
dialogue.text: It must have been a defense mechanism...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If we call the KUja pirate ship,

summary: This scene advances the Post-War / ASL Flashback conflict through Magellan, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12, Unknown person #9, focusing on the immediate stakes of luffy’s grief and the asl past: key exchange.
